detected_phrases: Comment @ mention system persistently failing despite multiple fix attempts
identical component behaves differently across contexts

failure_pattern_data.original_task: Implement @ mention functionality in CommentForm component identical to working PostCreator component

proposed_fixes: CRITICAL FIX: Direct MentionInput usage like QuickPost - clean layout
CRITICAL FIX: Follow QuickPost success pattern - simplify layout
CRITICAL FIX: Remove overlapping overlays that interfere with mention dropdown
CRITICAL FIX: Move formatting toolbar below input to prevent z-index conflicts
claude_solution.claimed_pattern: Apply working PostCreator pattern to CommentForm

success_component.name: PostCreator.tsx
success_component.behavior: Shows '🚨 EMERGENCY DEBUG: Dropdown Open' when typing @

failure_component.name: CommentForm.tsx
failure_component.behavior: NO debug dropdown visible when typing @

specific_failures.description: CommentForm uses complex nested layout with multiple stacking contexts and overlapping z-index values that prevent proper dropdown visibility
MentionService.getQuickMentions() returns different results for 'comment' vs 'post' context

evidence.comment_context_results: potentially fewer agents or different filtering

specific_failures.description: Comment form's complex DOM structure creates multiple stacking contexts that trap dropdown despite high z-index

stacking_contexts_created_by: nested div containers with relative positioning

prevention_strategies.implementation: Remove unnecessary nested containers, use direct MentionInput placement
Remove transform, backdrop-filter, isolation properties from parent containers
Use consistent z-index values: dropdown=1000, overlays=100-999, content=1-99

test_patterns.assertion: MentionInput dropdown appears in ALL components with @ typing
test_patterns.detection: look for '🚨 EMERGENCY DEBUG: Dropdown Open' message

test_patterns.assertion: Dropdown z-index actually works in component context
test_patterns.detection: dropdown element visible despite complex parent hierarchy

immediate_fixes.fix: Simplify DOM structure to match PostCreator.tsx success pattern
immediate_fixes.code_change: Remove nested div containers, place MentionInput directly in simple relative container

immediate_fixes.fix: Ensure consistent agent suggestions regardless of mentionContext value
immediate_fixes.code_change: Remove context-based filtering that discriminates against 'comment' context

structural_improvements.rule: All components using MentionInput should follow identical DOM hierarchy pattern
structural_improvements.enforcement: ESLint rule to detect complex nesting around MentionInput

structural_improvements.rule: All dropdowns use z-index: 1000, no stacking context creators in ancestors
structural_improvements.enforcement: CSS analysis to detect stacking context traps

similar_patterns.success_rate: 0% in complex layouts, 100% in flat layouts

prevention_metrics.tdd_usage_correlation: Components with dropdown TDD tests: 100% success rate

for_developers: Always test dropdown visibility in actual component context, not isolation
Use browser dev tools to inspect stacking contexts when dropdowns fail
Follow PostCreator.tsx as the canonical pattern for MentionInput integration
for_tdd_practitioners: Write cross-component dropdown visibility tests that detect context-dependent failures
Include CSS stacking context validation in dropdown component tests
Test identical components in different parent layouts to catch hierarchy bugs
for_architecture: Standardize component layout patterns to prevent context-dependent behavior
Implement CSS lint rules that detect dropdown-blocking patterns
Create component design guidelines that prevent stacking context traps

success_indicators: Flat DOM hierarchy (≤2 levels to MentionInput)
No transform/isolation properties in dropdown ancestors
Single z-index value for all dropdowns (1000)
Direct MentionInput placement without wrapper containers
